label: Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models

node.label: Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models
node.definition: Energy-based, Hopfield, and Boltzmann models assign scalar compatibility to configurations and perform recall or generation by moving toward lower-energy states.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models is modeled as depending on Energy-based objectives, Recurrence & feedback state and feeding downstream nodes such as Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection.
examples: atlas downstream link to Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Energy-based objectives, Recurrence & feedback state.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Energy-based objectives because Energy-based objectives is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Recurrence & feedback state.
In this atlas graph, Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models has a `composes` dependency on Differentiable memory read/write with `common` strength.
